ddls:
CREATE TABLE "food_products" (
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each food product */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the food category the product belongs to */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the food product */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the food product */,
  "production_date" TEXT /* Date when the product was produced */,
  "batch_number" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the production batch */,
  "expiry_date" TEXT /* Expiry date of the product */,
  "supplier_id" INTEGER /* ID of the supplier of raw materials */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the product (e.g., in production, quality check, ready for sale) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_food_products_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES food_categories ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_food_products_supplier_id FOREIGN KEY ("supplier_id") REFERENCES suppliers ("supplier_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "food_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each food category */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the food category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the food category */,
  "parent_category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent category (for hierarchical categories) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the category was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the category was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "texture_measurements" (
  "texture_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each texture measurement */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the food product the measurement belongs to */,
  "hardness" REAL /* Hardness value of the food product */,
  "chewiness" REAL /* Chewiness value of the food product */,
  "crispiness" REAL /* Crispiness value of the food product */,
  "measurement_date" TEXT /* Date when the texture measurement was taken */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor used for measurement */,
  "operator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operator who took the measurement */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or observations */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("texture_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_texture_measurements_operator_id FOREIGN KEY ("operator_id") REFERENCES operators ("operator_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_texture_measurements_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES food_products ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "color_measurements" (
  "color_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each color measurement */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the food product the measurement belongs to */,
  "l_star" REAL /* L* value of the food product */,
  "a_star" REAL /* a* value of the food product */,
  "b_star" REAL /* b* value of the food product */,
  "measurement_date" TEXT /* Date when the color measurement was taken */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor used for measurement */,
  "operator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operator who took the measurement */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or observations */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("color_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_color_measurements_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES food_products ("product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_color_measurements_operator_id FOREIGN KEY ("operator_id") REFERENCES operators ("operator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "taste_measurements" (
  "taste_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each taste measurement */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the food product the measurement belongs to */,
  "sweetness" REAL /* Sweetness value of the food product */,
  "saltiness" REAL /* Saltiness value of the food product */,
  "bitterness" REAL /* Bitterness value of the food product */,
  "measurement_date" TEXT /* Date when the taste measurement was taken */,
  "taster_id" INTEGER /* ID of the taster who evaluated the taste */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or observations */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("taste_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_taste_measurements_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES food_products ("product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_taste_measurements_taster_id FOREIGN KEY ("taster_id") REFERENCES tasters ("taster_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "shape_measurements" (
  "shape_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each shape measurement */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the food product the measurement belongs to */,
  "diameter" REAL /* Diameter value of the food product */,
  "thickness" REAL /* Thickness value of the food product */,
  "measurement_date" TEXT /* Date when the shape measurement was taken */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor used for measurement */,
  "operator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operator who took the measurement */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or observations */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("shape_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_shape_measurements_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES food_products ("product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_shape_measurements_operator_id FOREIGN KEY ("operator_id") REFERENCES operators ("operator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "smell_measurements" (
  "smell_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each smell measurement */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the food product the measurement belongs to */,
  "intensity" REAL /* Intensity value of the food product */,
  "complexity" REAL /* Complexity value of the food product */,
  "specific_compound" TEXT /* Specific compound detected in the food product */,
  "measurement_date" TEXT /* Date when the smell measurement was taken */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor used for measurement */,
  "operator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operator who took the measurement */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or observations */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("smell_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_smell_measurements_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES food_products ("product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_smell_measurements_operator_id FOREIGN KEY ("operator_id") REFERENCES operators ("operator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "defect_detection" (
  "defect_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each defect detection result */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the food product the result belongs to */,
  "defect_type" TEXT /* Type of defect detected (e.g., mold, bruise) */,
  "defect_level" REAL /* Level of defect detected (e.g., low, high) */,
  "detection_date" TEXT /* Date when the defect was detected */,
  "operator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operator who detected the defect */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or observations */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("defect_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_defect_detection_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES food_products ("product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_defect_detection_operator_id FOREIGN KEY ("operator_id") REFERENCES operators ("operator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., quality control manager, production team member) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user was last updated */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the user account is active */,
  "is_admin" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the user has administrative privileges */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "suppliers" (
  "supplier_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each supplier */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the supplier */,
  "contact_person" TEXT /* Contact person at the supplier */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the supplier */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the supplier */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the supplier */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the supplier was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the supplier was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("supplier_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "operators" (
  "operator_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each operator */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the operator */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the operator */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the operator */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the operator was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the operator was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("operator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tasters" (
  "taster_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each taster */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the taster */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the taster */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the taster was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the taster was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("taster_id")
)
